concepts.title: Human Development Index (HDI)
concepts.description: The Human Development Index (HDI) is a composite statistic of life expectancy, education, and per capita income indicators, which are used to rank countries into four tiers of human development.

concepts.title: Dimensions of Human Development
concepts.description: The three dimensions of human development are: (i) A long and healthy life (life expectancy), (ii) knowledge (literacy rate and educational attainment), and (iii) a decent standard of living (GDP per capita).

concepts.title: Gross National Income (GNI) vs. Gross National Product (GNP)
concepts.description: Gross National Income (GNI) is the total income earned by the citizens of a country, while Gross National Product (GNP) is the total value of goods and services produced within a country's borders, including income earned by foreign residents.

concepts.title: Poverty Line
concepts.description: To remember the poverty line, use the mnemonic: 'Poverty Line is the Line Below which People Live in Poverty' (PLILBPLIP).

concepts.title: Confusing HDI with GDP
concepts.description: Students often confuse HDI with GDP. Remember, HDI is a composite index of life expectancy, education, and per capita income, while GDP is a measure of the total value of goods and services produced within a country's borders.

concepts.title: UN Development Goals
concepts.description: The UN Development Goals include the Millennium Development Goals (MDGs) and the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). The SDGs are a set of 17 goals adopted by the UN in 2015 to end poverty, protect the planet, and ensure peace and prosperity by 2030.

concepts.title: Demographic Dividend
concepts.description: Demographic dividend is the economic benefit of a country when the proportion of working-age population is higher than the proportion of dependent population (children and elderly).

concepts.title: Demographic Transition Model
concepts.description: The demographic transition model describes the changes in population growth rates as a country develops from a pre-industrial to an industrialized economy, with four stages: high birth and death rates, declining birth and death rates, low birth and death rates, and low birth rate and low death rate.
